report_metadata.report_title: AR25-261A Malicious Listener for Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile Systems

http_paths.evidence_snippet: The cyber threat actors targeted the /mifs/rs/api/v2/ endpoint with HTTP GET requests

cves.evidence_snippet: cyber threat actors exploited CVE-2025-4427 [CWE-288: Authentication Bypass Using an Alternate Path or Channel]
and CVE-2025-4428 [CWE-‘Code Injection’] in Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (Ivanti EPMM) deployments for initial access

yara_rules.name: CISA_25_112601_trojan_hides_artifacts
yara_rules.evidence_snippet: rule CISA_25_112601_trojan_hides_artifacts... description Detects malicious jar filter samples

yara_rules.name: CISA_25_112602_trojan
yara_rules.evidence_snippet: rule CISA_25_112602_trojan... description Detects malicious servlet filter class loader samples

yara_rules.evidence_snippet: rule CISA_25_112604_trojan_hides_artifacts... description Detects malicious jar Tomcat listener shell samples

sigma_rules.name: Loader_Malware_for_Ivanti_Mobile_Management_Systems
sigma_rules.evidence_snippet: title Detects Artifacts Based on MAR-251126, Ivanti EPMM CVE-2025-4427 and CVE-2025-4428

ttps.technique: Obfuscated Files or Information: Compile After Delivery
ttps.evidence_snippet: The cyber threat actors delivered malware in segments, splitting it into multiple Base64-encoded segments

ttps.technique: Obfuscated Files or Information: Embedded Payloads
Obfuscated Files or Information: Encrypted/Encoded File
ttps.evidence_snippet: WebAndroidAppInstaller.class AES encrypts and Base64 encodes a class output with a hard coded key
Loader 1 masquerades ReflectUtil.class as part of the org.apache.http package
ReflectUtil.class reflectively loads code into Java processes to add a malicious listener to Apache Tomcat servers
ReflectUtil.class decodes and decompresses a class bytecode
WebAndroidAppInstaller.class decodes a Base64 password parameter value using an AES algorithm and hard-coded key

summary: CISA malware analysis of two malicious listener sets targeting Ivanti EPMM systems exploiting CVE-2025-4427 (authentication bypass) and CVE-2025-4428 (code injection). Around May 15, 2025, threat actors chained vulnerabilities via /mifs/rs/api/v2/ endpoint, deployed Base64-encoded JAR loaders (web-install.jar) containing malicious listeners (SecurityHandlerWanListener.class, WebAndroidAppInstaller.class) enabling arbitrary code execution, data exfiltration via HTTP interception, AES encryption with hard-coded keys. Includes YARA/SIGMA detection signatures.

timeline.event: Ivanti provided patch and disclosed CVE-2025-4427 and CVE-2025-4428
timeline.evidence_snippet: Ivanti provided a patch and disclosed the vulnerabilities on May 13, 2025

timeline.event: Cyber threat actors gained access following proof of concept publication
timeline.evidence_snippet: Around May 15, 2025, following publication of a proof of concept, the cyber threat actors gained access

timeline.event: CISA added CVE-2025-4427 and CVE-2025-4428 to KEV Catalog
timeline.evidence_snippet: CISA added both vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog on May 19, 2025
